title: gratiola georgiana
intro: Gratiola georgiana, also known as Georgia's gold, is a plant species in the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to the southeastern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Gratiola georgiana include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate headaches. Gratiola georgiana can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote respiratory health and relieve symptoms associated with colds and flu.
